title: nephrolepis lunulata
intro: Nephrolepis lunulata, also known as the sword fern or rabbit's foot fern, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly found in tropical and subtropical regions. The leaves of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Nephrolepis lunulata include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support wound healing due to its antiseptic and antifungal properties. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often dried and used in teas, infusions, and topical applications, although its safety and efficacy for human consumption and use have not been extensively scientifically studied.
